axioms.name: Drama as Mimicry
axioms.statement: Drama (Natya) is mimicry (anukarana) of the exploits of gods, Asuras, kings, and householders.

axioms.name: Sentiment as Soul
axioms.statement: The Sentiment (rasa) is the soul of drama—no meaning proceeds without it.

axioms.name: Aesthetic Tasting
axioms.statement: Aesthetic experience is 'tasted' (asvadayanti)—like culinary flavor from combined ingredients.

axioms.name: Dual Practice
axioms.statement: Successful representation requires both realistic (lokadharmi) and conventional (natyadharmi) practices.

levels.name: ACT
levels.description: Major structural division containing single-day incidents

levels.name: SANDHI
levels.description: One of five junctures linking plot stages

levels.name: BEAT
levels.description: Atomic unit of performance (gesture, word, cue)

core_algorithms.name: Rasa Evocation Formula
core_algorithms.purpose: Produce universal aesthetic experience through combined states
core_algorithms.pseudocode: rasa = STHAYIBHAVA + VIBHAVA + ANUBHAVA + VYABHICARI; // Combine Dominant State with Determinants, Consequents, and Transitory States through Abhinaya

core_algorithms.name: Performance-Integrated Architecture
core_algorithms.purpose: Integrate musical and vocal cues into the narrative flow
core_algorithms.pseudocode: IF character_entrance: PLAY praveśikī; IF scene_transition: PLAY antarā; IF emotional_resolution: PLAY prāsādikī; SYNC dhruva TO stage_action;

core_algorithms.name: Spatial and Kinetic Grammar
core_algorithms.purpose: Represent story world distance and character identity through movement
core_algorithms.pseudocode: 1. Identify character_type; 2. SET base_gait (Gati); 3. MODIFY for emotional_state; 4. ASSIGN stage_zone (Kakṣyā); 5. MAP walking_steps TO narrative_distance;

core_algorithms.name: Content Admissibility Filter
core_algorithms.purpose: Enforce on-stage prohibitions while maintaining narrative continuity
core_algorithms.pseudocode: IF event IN [death_of_hero, battle, eating, sexual_union]: CONVERT TO report_in_praveśaka; ELSE: SHOW_WITH_CONVENTION;

diagnostic_questions.question: Which Dominant State (sthayibhava) is being evoked?
Are the determinants (causes) and consequents (expressions) appropriate to the rasa?
Does character gait (Gati) match their social type and emotional state?
Is character position on stage (Kakshya) used symbolically to represent distance or status?
Are prohibited actions (death, battle) reported rather than shown directly?
Does the musical cue (Dhruva) correctly signal the narrative beat (entrance, exit, transition)?

maps_to: McKee's Value Turn (Implied in Bhava shifts)

key_constraints: Rasa is the soul; no meaning without it
Act must contain only single-day events
